title: Simplify finance graph — marketing-budget unit talk-track
html:
TL;DR

  • Talk-track scaffolding for an FBLA-era finance/marketing presentation: digest the graph, explain how marketing budget grows over time, name the customer, surface the indirect link between spend and sales.
  • Names the misconception of more produced equals more sales — "we bet our money on it" — Yahir's earliest articulation of a supply-side fallacy that he keeps revisiting as a frame.
  • Closes with the line "a lot on our plate, but we have to feast" — a fragment of the aphorism tic that runs through the teen era.
  • Lives next to the keep things simple note (4 days earlier) and the credentials note from the day before — a tight Aug 15-16 cluster of Yahir prepping a presentation, probably for FBLA state-prep or summer business-class capstone.
  • Concept seed: the indirect-link-between-spend-and-outcome frame — a pattern Yahir will reach for again in the 2020 SEO/AI thesis and the 2025 Rankett pricing rationale.

Notes

Tiny note (six lines) but it's an outline, not a journal — Yahir is rehearsing slide bullets in his head. The structure:

  1. Digest the graph into a summary
  2. Explain how over-time marketing budget increases
  3. Misconception of more produced = more sales. We bet our money on it.
  4. A lot on our plate, but we have to feast
  5. Emphasize who the customer really is
  6. Explain the indirect link

The "indirect link" line is the load-bearing one — it's the same instinct that becomes the AI visibility argument six years later (you don't measure the link directly, you measure proxies for it). The "who the customer really is" line is also early Yahir-thinking — right question framing applied to marketing.

The "feast" metaphor recurs: a year of "a lot on our plate" notes through 2019 (2019 12 12 talk to brenda is the canonical mega-list). The kid is loading up his plate on purpose.

Open questions

  • Which class / competition was this for? Likely FBLA marketing or business-presentation event, given the August timing (state season is spring, but summer is prep).
  • Is "who the customer really is" a Brenda-coached line or a Dale Carnegie read-back? The phrasing has the same cadence as the credentials-list from the day before.

Connections

  • 2019 08 12 keep things simple — 4 days earlier, same prep-mode
  • 2019 08 15 credentials and ceo maxims — day-before sibling
  • 2019 08 15 youve got to have some sort of credentials — same-day variant
  • FBLA · grants pass teen era · asking the right question
